title: task
description: python/src/lichen/rpl/trickle.py:127-131 - If reset() is called from another coroutine during 'await transmit()', the new interval started by reset() has its transmit opportunity skipped. After transmit() completes, the run loop sleeps to interval_end (now the NEW interval's end) then calls expire() without ever evaluating whether to transmit in the reset interval. Failure scenario: Node receives inconsistent message during DIO transmission, calls reset(). The reset interval's transmit at [I/2, I) is never evaluated; instead the loop immediately expires to the next interval. This causes one missed DIO transmission opportunity.
close_reason: Fixed: Added generation counter to detect reset() calls during await points in run() loop. Loop now restarts the interval evaluation when generation changes, ensuring reset intervals get their transmit opportunity evaluated.
